programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the 20212 final at Wembley Stadium. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW
programme.desc: I left here as a boy and I'm definitely coming back as a man. With 25 goals in Aston Villa's promotion-winning Championship season in 2018/19, Tammy Abraham still holds a special place in the hearts of Villa fans. Having risen through the ranks at Chelsea but then moved on by Thomas Tuchel, a determination to succeed saw him win plaudits wherever he played - For Roma and AC Milan in Serie A and lately for Besiktas, but Abraham has unfinished business in the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We speak to Dimitar Berbatov, a player whose elegance and composure left a lasting imprint on English football. Focusing on his Premier League years, we follow his rise at Tottenham Hotspur, where his effortless style first captivated fans, through to his trophy-laden spell at Manchester United, where moments of brilliance were often matched by intense scrutiny.

programme.title: 1-TO-11
programme.desc: Known simply as 'The Yak,' Yakubu takes on 1-to-11, selecting his ultimate lineup from the best players he shared the pitch with. A powerhouse striker with 95 Premier League goals across spells at Portsmouth, Middlesbrough, Everton, and Blackburn, he reflects on the teammates who made the biggest impression during his career. Expect a team full of strength, flair, and goalscoring pedigree.

programme.title: PL STORIES
programme.desc: We sit down with Duncan Ferguson to discuss the fierce, passionate, and often unpredictable career of one of Everton's most legendary figures. From his early days in Scotland to his turbulent arrival in English football, PL Stories focuses on Ferguson's deep bond with the blue half of Merseyside; a connection built on loyalty, grit, and unforgettable moments. Through highs, lows, goals, and red cards, we explore how he became more than just a player to Evertonians...

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW
programme.desc: At just 21 years old, Alejandro Garnacho has already experienced more in his young career than many others do in a lifetime. Born in the Spanish captial, Garnacho started his career in the Atletico Madrid academy. Released as a teenager, the Argentina international moved to Manchester United, where he went on to make nearly 100 senior appearances for the club over three years before joining Chelsea in the summer.

Having risen through the ranks at Chelsea but then moved on by Thomas Tuchel, a determination to succeed saw him win plaudits wherever he played - For Roma and AC Milan in Serie A and lately for Besiktas, but Abraham has unfinished business in the Premier League.

programme.title: Premier League 2025/26
programme.desc: Wolves are unbeaten in their last seven home league games against Sunderland (W3 D4). This is the first time they’ve hosted them since a 0-0 draw in the Championship in December 2017, and first time in the Premier League since December 2011 (2-1).

programme.title: PL Rewind 2025/26
programme.desc: One of the most dramatic survival stories in Premier League history, West Ham stayed up against all odds in the 2006/07 season. Robert Green was between the posts for the Irons, and tells the story of their "Great Escape".

programme.title: PL Stories 2025/26
programme.desc: We speak to Dimitar Berbatov, a player whose elegance and composure left a lasting imprint on English football. Focusing on his Premier League years, we follow his rise at Tottenham Hotspur, where his effortless style first captivated fans, through to his trophy-laden spell at Manchester United, where moments of brilliance were often matched by intense scrutiny.

programme.title: The Art Of... 2025/26
programme.desc: Once the unsung workhorses of English football, centre-backs have become some of the Premier League’s most influential figures. This week, The Art of... sits down with two-time Premier League winner Joleon Lescott to explore how the role has evolved from last-ditch defending to a position that now shapes games, systems and titles. Joleon breaks down some of the key attributes of the role, and examines how the duties of the centre-back have expanded over time.
